title: The Life of a Showgirl
description: Vibrant pop celebrating fame and love with showgirl flair

songs.title: The Fate of Ophelia
songs.lyrics:
I heard you calling on the megaphone
You wanna see me all alone
As legend has it, you are quite the pyro
You light the match to watch it blow

And if you'd never come for me
I might've drowned in the melancholy
I swore my loyalty to me (Me), myself (Myself), and I (I)
Right before you lit my sky up

All that time
I sat alone in my tower
You were just honing your powers
Now I can see it all (See it all)
Late one night
You dug me out of my grave and
Saved my heart from the fate of
Ophelia (Ophеlia)

Keep it one hundred on the land (Land), thе sea (Sea), the sky
Pledge allegiance to your hands, your team, your vibes
Don't care where the hell you've been (Been) 'cause now (Now) you're mine
It's 'bout to be the sleepless night you've been dreaming of
The fate of Ophelia

The eldest daughter of a nobleman
Ophelia lived in fantasy
But love was a cold bed full of scorpions
The venom stole her sanity

And if you'd never come for me (Come for me)
I might've lingered in purgatory
You wrap around me like a chain (A chain), a crown (A crown), a vine (A vine)
Pulling me into the fire

All that time
I sat alone in my tower
You were just honing your powers
Now I can see it all (See it all)
Late one night
You dug me out of my grave and
Saved my heart from the fate of
Ophelia (Ophelia)

Keep it one hundred on the land (Land), the sea (The sea), the sky
Pledge allegiance to your hands, your team, your vibes
Don't care where the hell you've been (Been) 'cause now (Now) you're mine
It's 'bout to be the sleepless night you've been dreaming of
The fate of Ophelia

'Tis locked inside my memory
And only you possess the key
No longer drowning and deceived
All because you came for me
Locked inside my memory
And only you possess the key
No longer drowning and deceived
All because you came for me

All that time
I sat alone in my tower
You were just honing your powers
Now I can see it all (I can see it all)
Late one night
You dug me out of my grave and
Saved my heart from the fate of
Ophelia (Ophelia)

Keep it one hundred on the land (Land), the sea (The sea), the sky
Pledge allegiance to your hands (Your hands), your team, your vibes
Don't care where the hell you've been (You've been) 'cause now ('Cause now) you're mine
It's 'bout to be the sleepless night you've been dreaming of
The fate of Ophelia

You saved my heart from the fate of Ophelia

vocab.definition: a person who uses fire or explosives for entertainment or destructive purposes
vocab.lyric_snippet: As legend has it, you are quite the pyro
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone who is reckless and destructive, but also charismatic and attention-grabbing.

vocab.definition: a feeling of sadness or sorrow, often accompanied by a sense of hopelessness
vocab.lyric_snippet: I might've drowned in the melancholy
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a state of emotional despair, suggesting that she was on the verge of giving up without the person's intervention.

vocab.definition: a state of suffering or torment, often used to describe a transitional or liminal state
vocab.lyric_snippet: I might've lingered in purgatory
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a state of emotional limbo, suggesting that she was stuck between two states of being without the person's intervention.

vocab.definition: a toxic or poisonous substance, often used metaphorically to describe a harmful or corrupting influence
vocab.lyric_snippet: But love was a cold bed full of scorpions
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the corrupting influence of love, suggesting that it can be a source of harm or suffering.

vocab.definition: a pledge of loyalty or devotion, often used to describe a commitment to a person, cause, or idea
vocab.lyric_snippet: Pledge allegiance to your hands, your team, your vibes
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a commitment to the person, suggesting that she is willing to put their needs and desires above her own.

vocab.definition: an archaic or poetic way of saying 'it is'
vocab.lyric_snippet: 'Tis locked inside my memory
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to create a sense of antiquity or nostalgia, suggesting that the memory is old and deeply ingrained.

songs.title: Elizabeth Taylor
songs.lyrics:
Elizabeth Taylor
Do you think it's forever?

That view of Portofino was on my mind when you called me at the Plaza Athénée
Ooh-ooh, oftentimes it doesn't feel so glamorous to be me
All the right guys promised they'd stay
Under bright lights, they withered away
But you bloom
Portofino was on my mind (And I think you know why)

And if your letters ever said, "Goodbye"

I'd cry my eyes violet, Elizabeth Taylor
Tell me for real, do you think it's forever?
Been number one, but I never had two
And I can't have fun if I can't have— (Uh)
Be my NY whеn Hollywood hates me
You're only as hot as your last hit, baby
Been numbеr one, but I never had two
And I can't have fun if I can't have you

Hey-ey, what could you possibly get for the girl who has everything and nothing all at once?
Babe, I would trade the Cartier for someone to trust (Just kidding)
We hit the best booth at Musso and Frank's
They say I'm bad news, I just say, "Thanks"
And you
Look at me like you're hypnotized, and I think you know why

And if you ever leave me high and dry

I'd cry my eyes violet, Elizabeth Taylor
Tell me for real, do you think it's forever?
Been number one, but I never had two
And I can't have fun if I can't have— (Uh)
Be my NY when Hollywood hates me
You're only as hot as your last hit, baby
Been number one, but I never had two
And I can't have fun if I can't have (Uh) you

Elizabeth Taylor (Oh)
Do you think it's forever? (Oh)
If I can't have you

(Ah) All my white diamonds and lovers are forever
(Ah) In the papers, on the screen, and in their minds
(Ah) All my white diamonds and lovers are forever
(Ah) Don't you ever end up anything but mine

I'd cry my eyes violet, Elizabeth Taylor
Tell me for real, do you think it's forever?
Been number one, but I never had two
And I can't have fun if I can't have— (You)
Be my NY when Hollywood hates me
You're only as hot as your last hit, baby
Been number one, but I never had two
And I can't have fun if I can't have— (You)

All my white diamonds and lovers are forever (Elizabeth Taylor)
(Do you think it's forever?) In the papers, on the screen, and in their minds
All my white diamonds and lovers are forever
Don't you ever end up anything but mine, oh

vocab.definition: used to indicate that something happens frequently or regularly
vocab.lyric_snippet: Ooh-ooh, oftentimes it doesn't feel so glamorous to be me
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to express her feelings of glamour being fleeting and not always present in her life.

vocab.definition: to become weak or feeble, especially because of lack of food or water
vocab.lyric_snippet: Under bright lights, they withered away
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe how people can be affected by the pressures of fame and the spotlight.

vocab.definition: a deep purple color
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'd cry my eyes violet, Elizabeth Taylor
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the intensity of her emotions and the vividness of her imagination.

vocab.definition: a quantity or amount
vocab.lyric_snippet: Been number one, but I never had two
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her position in the music industry and her desire for more.

vocab.definition: to be in a state of trance or deep sleep
vocab.lyric_snippet: Look at me like you're hypnotized, and I think you know why
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the way she feels when she's with the person she loves.

vocab.definition: a French luxury goods conglomerate that produces high-end jewelry and watches
vocab.lyric_snippet: Babe, I would trade the Cartier for someone to trust
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the material possessions she has, but values trust and relationships more.

vocab.definition: morally wrong or wicked
vocab.lyric_snippet: They say I'm bad news, I just say, 'Thanks'
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the negative rumors and perceptions about her, but she doesn't let it affect her.

vocab.definition: for an indefinitely long time
vocab.lyric_snippet: Do you think it's forever?
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her desire for a long-lasting relationship and her fear of losing it.

vocab.definition: New York City
vocab.lyric_snippet: Be my NY when Hollywood hates me
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her desire for a place where she can escape the pressures of fame and be herself.

songs.title: Opalite
songs.lyrics:
I had a bad habit
Of missing lovers past
My brother used to call it
"Eating out of the trash"
It's never gonna last
I thought my house was haunted
I used to live with ghosts
And all the perfect couples
Said, "When you know, you know"
And, "When you don't, you don't"

And all of the foes, and all of the friends (Ha, ha)
Have seen it before, they'll see it again (Ha, ha)
Life is a song, it ends when it ends
I was wrong

But my mama told me, "It's alright
You were dancing through the lightning strikes
Sleepless in the onyx night
But now, the sky is opalite, oh-oh, oh, oh, oh
Oh, my Lord
Never made no one like you bеfore
You had to make your own sunshine
But now, thе sky is opalite, oh-oh, oh, oh, oh, oh"

You couldn't understand it
Why you felt alone
You were in it for real
She was in her phone
And you were just a pose
And don't we try to love love? (Love, love)
We give it all we got (Give it all we got)
You finally left the table (Uh, uh)
And what a simple thought
You're starving 'til you're not

And all of the foes, and all of the friends (Ha, ha)
Have messed up before, they'll mess up again (Ha, ha)
Life is a song, it ends when it ends
You move on

And that's when I told you, "It's alright
You were dancing through the lightning strikes
Sleepless in the onyx night
But now, the sky is opalite, oh-oh, oh, oh, oh
Oh, my Lord
Never met no one like you before
You had to make your own sunshine
But now, the sky is opalite, oh-oh, oh, oh, oh, oh"

This is just
A storm inside a teacup
But shelter here with me, my love
Thunder like a drum
This life will beat you up, up, up, up
This is just
A temporary speed bump
But failure brings you freedom
And I can bring you love, love, love, love (Love)

Don't you sweat it, baby, it's alright
You were dancing through the lightning strikes
Oh, so sleepless in the onyx night
But now, the sky is opalite, oh-oh, oh, oh, oh
Oh, my Lord
Never met no one like you before (No)
You had to make your own sunshine
But now, the sky is opalite, oh-oh, oh, oh, oh, oh

vocab.definition: a type of gemstone, often used to describe something dark and mysterious
vocab.lyric_snippet: Sleepless in the onyx night
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'onyx' to describe the darkness of the night, emphasizing the contrast with the 'opalite' sky

vocab.definition: a way of behaving or presenting oneself, often superficially
vocab.lyric_snippet: And you were just a pose
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'pose' to describe someone who is pretending to be something they're not, highlighting the superficiality of relationships

vocab.definition: a small cup used for drinking tea, often used metaphorically to describe something fragile or delicate
vocab.lyric_snippet: This is just a storm inside a teacup
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'teacup' to describe the turmoil in a relationship, emphasizing its fragility and delicacy

vocab.definition: the rate at which something moves or happens
vocab.lyric_snippet: A temporary speed bump
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'speed' to describe the temporary setbacks in life, emphasizing the idea that they are temporary and can be overcome

vocab.definition: the state of being free from constraints or limitations
vocab.lyric_snippet: But failure brings you freedom
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'freedom' to describe the liberating effect of failure, emphasizing the idea that it can lead to personal growth and empowerment

vocab.definition: a place of safety or refuge
vocab.lyric_snippet: But shelter here with me, my love
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'shelter' to describe the safety and comfort of being in a loving relationship, emphasizing the idea of finding refuge in someone else

vocab.definition: a musical instrument, often used to describe a loud or insistent sound
vocab.lyric_snippet: Thunder like a drum
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'drum' to describe the sound of thunder, emphasizing its loud and intense quality

vocab.definition: relating to time, especially in a way that is temporary or fleeting
vocab.lyric_snippet: A temporary speed bump
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'temporal' to describe the temporary nature of setbacks in life, emphasizing the idea that they are fleeting and can be overcome

vocab.definition: a word or phrase that is the opposite of another word or phrase
vocab.lyric_snippet: Never met no one like you before
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'opposite' to describe the uniqueness of the person she is singing about, emphasizing the idea that they are unlike anyone else

songs.title: Father Figure
songs.lyrics:
When I found you, you were young, wayward, lost in the cold
Pulled up to you in the Jag', turned your rags into gold
The winding road leads to the chateau
"You remind me of a younger me," I saw potential

I'll be your father figure, I drink that brown liquor
I can make deals with the devil because my dick's bigger
This love is pure profit, just step into my office
I dry your tears with my sleeve

Leave it with me, I protect the family
Leave it with me, I protect the family

I pay the check before it kisses the mahogany grain
Said, "They wanna see you rise, they don't want you to reign"
I showed you all the tricks of the trade
All I asked for is your loyalty, my dear protégé

I'll be your father figure, I drink that brown liquor
I can make deals with the devil because my dick's bigger
This love is pure profit, just step into my office
They'll know your name in the streets

Leave it with me, I protect the family

I saw a change in you  (I saw a change, saw a change in you)
My dear boy
They don't make loyalty like they used to (Not like they used to)
Your thoughtless ambition sparked the ignition
On foolish decisions, which led to misguided visions
That to fulfill your dreams
You had to get rid of me
I protect the family

I was your father figure, we drank that brown liquor
You made a deal with this devil, turns out my dick's bigger
You want a fight? You found it, I got the place surrounded
You'll be sleeping with the fishes before you know you're drowning
Whose portrait's on the mantel? Who covered up your scandals?
Mistake my kindness for weakness and find your card cancelled
I was your father figure, you pulled the wrong trigger
This empire belongs to me

Leave it with me, I protect the family
Leave it with me, I protect the family
Leave it with me

"You know, you remind me of a younger me"
I saw potential

vocab.definition: A wayward person is someone who is wandering or moving without a clear direction, often in a reckless or irresponsible manner.
vocab.lyric_snippet: When I found you, you were young, wayward, lost in the cold
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'wayward' to describe the person's youthful and possibly reckless state.

vocab.definition: A large, luxurious house, especially one built in the countryside, often with a grand and impressive appearance.
vocab.lyric_snippet: The winding road leads to the chateau
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'chateau' to describe a symbol of wealth and luxury.

vocab.definition: A person who is being protected, trained, or sponsored by someone more experienced or influential, often in a professional or artistic context.
vocab.lyric_snippet: All I asked for is your loyalty, my dear protégé
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'protégé' to describe the person's role as a mentee or apprentice.

vocab.definition: The act or process of starting or sparking something, such as a fire, an engine, or a passion.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Your thoughtless ambition sparked the ignition
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'ignition' to describe the spark or catalyst that sets something in motion.

vocab.definition: Lacking direction or purpose, often due to poor judgment or a lack of understanding.
vocab.lyric_snippet: On foolish decisions, which led to misguided visions
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'misguided' to describe the person's flawed or incorrect decisions.

vocab.definition: A shelf or ledge, often in a fireplace or a large room, used to display decorative objects or family portraits.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Whose portrait's on the mantel?
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'mantel' to describe a symbol of family legacy and tradition.

vocab.definition: Formally or officially ended or stopped, often due to a lack of progress or a failure to meet expectations.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Mistake my kindness for weakness and find your card cancelled
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'cancelled' to describe the person's loss of status or influence.

songs.title: Eldest Daughter
songs.lyrics:
Everybody's so punk on the internet
Everyone's unbothered 'til they're not
Every joke's just trolling and memes
Sad as it seems, apathy is hot
Everybody's cutthroat in the comments
Every single hot take is cold as ice
When you found me, I said I was busy
That was a lie

I have been afflicted by a terminal uniqueness
I've been dying just from trying to seem cool

But I'm not a bad bitch
And this isn't savage
But I'm never gonna let you down
I'm never gonna leave you out
So many traitors
Smooth operators
But I'm never gonna break that vow
I'm nеver gonna leave you now, now, now

You know, thе last time I laughed this hard was
On the trampoline in somebody's backyard
I must've been about eight or nine
That was the night I fell off and broke my arm
Pretty soon, I learned cautious discretion
When your first crush crushes something kind
When I said I don't believe in marriage
That was a lie

Every eldest daughter was the first lamb to the slaughter
So we all dressed up as wolves and we looked fire

But I'm not a bad bitch
And this isn't savage
But I'm never gonna let you down
I'm never gonna leave you out
So many traitors
Smooth operators
But I'm never gonna break that vow
I'm never gonna leave you now, now, now

We lie back
A beautiful, beautiful time-lapse
Ferris wheels, kisses, and lilacs
And things I said were dumb
'Cause I thought that I'd never find that
Beautiful, beautiful life that
Shimmers that innocent light back
Like when we were young

Every youngest child felt
They were raised up in the wild
But now you're home

'Cause I'm not a bad bitch
And this isn't savage
And I'm never gonna let you down
I'm never gonna leave you out
So many traitors
Smooth operators
But I'm never gonna break that vow (Never gonna break that vow)
I'm never gonna leave you now, now, now

Never gonna break that vow (Oh)
I'm never gonna leave you now, now
I'm never gonna leave you now

vocab.definition: untroubled or unaffected by something; indifferent
vocab.lyric_snippet: Everyone's unbothered 'til they're not
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe people's apathy and lack of concern for others on the internet.

vocab.definition: the act of posting inflammatory or provocative messages online to elicit a reaction
vocab.lyric_snippet: Every joke's just trolling and memes
vocab.context: Taylor criticizes the online behavior of people who engage in trolling to provoke reactions.

vocab.definition: a lack of interest, concern, or enthusiasm
vocab.lyric_snippet: Sad as it seems, apathy is hot
vocab.context: Taylor observes that apathy has become a popular or trendy attitude on the internet.

vocab.definition: ruthless or competitive, often in a way that is considered unfair or unscrupulous
vocab.lyric_snippet: Everybody's cutthroat in the comments
vocab.context: Taylor describes the aggressive and competitive nature of online comments.

vocab.definition: extreme or absolute; used to describe a condition or situation that is severe or irreversible
vocab.lyric_snippet: I've been afflicted by a terminal uniqueness
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the pressure to be unique and stand out, which can be overwhelming.

vocab.definition: sophisticated or charming, often in a way that is considered insincere or manipulative
vocab.lyric_snippet: So many smooth operators
vocab.context: Taylor describes people who are charming or manipulative, often in a way that is considered insincere.

vocab.definition: careful or prudent, often in a way that is considered overly cautious or risk-averse
vocab.lyric_snippet: Pretty soon, I learned cautious discretion
vocab.context: Taylor describes learning to be careful and cautious in her relationships, particularly when it comes to romantic crushes.

vocab.definition: the quality of being careful or prudent in one's actions or decisions
vocab.lyric_snippet: Pretty soon, I learned cautious discretion
vocab.context: Taylor describes learning to be careful and cautious in her relationships, particularly when it comes to romantic crushes.

vocab.definition: the oldest child in a family
vocab.lyric_snippet: Every eldest daughter was the first lamb to the slaughter
vocab.context: Taylor references the idea that the eldest child often bears a disproportionate amount of responsibility or burden.

vocab.definition: ruthless or brutal, often in a way that is considered cruel or heartless
vocab.lyric_snippet: But this isn't savage
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe behavior that is considered cruel or heartless, but then rejects the idea that she is engaging in such behavior.

songs.title: Ruin the Friendship
songs.lyrics:
Glistening grass from September rain
Gray overpass full of neon names
You drive (Mm, mm)
Eighty-five (Mm, mm)
Gallatin Road and the Lakeside Beach
Watching the game from your brother's Jeep
Your smile (Mm, mm)
Miles wide

And it was not an invitation
Should've kissed you anyway
Should've kissed you anyway
And it was not convenient, no
But your girlfriend was away
Should've kissed you anyway, hey

Shiny wood floors underneath my feet
Disco ball makes everything look cheap
Have fun (Mm, mm)
It's prom (Mm, mm)
Wilted corsage dangles from my wrist
Over his shoulder, I catch a glimpse
And see (Mm, mm)
You looking at mе

And it was not an invitation
But as the 50 Cent song played (Song playеd)
Should've kissed you anyway (Anyway)
And it was not (And it was not) convenient (Convenient), no
Would've been the best mistake
Should've kissed you anyway, hey

Don't make it awkward in second period
Might piss your ex off, lately, we've been good
Staying friends is safe, doesn't mean you should
Don't make it awkward in second period
Might piss your ex off, lately, we've been good
Staying friends is safe, doesn't mean you should

When I left school, I lost track of you
Abigail called me with the bad news
Goodbye
And we'll never know why

It was not an invitation
But I flew home anyway
With so much left to say
It was not convenient, no
But I whispered at the grave
"Should've kissed you anyway," ooh
And it was not (It was not) an invitation (Invitation)
Should've kissed you anyway (Anyway)
Should've kissed you anyway (Anyway), anyway
And it was not—

My advice is always ruin the friendship
Better that than regret it for all time
Should've kissed you anyway
And my advice is always answer the question
Better that than to ask it all your life
Should've kissed you anyway

Should've kissed you anyway

vocab.definition: glistening means giving off a shining or sparkling appearance, especially due to being wet or covered in dew.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Glistening grass from September rain
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'glistening' to describe the appearance of the grass after a September rain, setting a nostalgic and dreamy tone for the song.

vocab.definition: convenient means suitable or favorable for a particular purpose or situation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And it was not convenient, no
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'convenient' to express that the situation of kissing someone's brother was not a suitable or favorable one, highlighting the awkwardness and uncertainty.

vocab.definition: wilted means having lost freshness, vitality, or strength, often due to being exposed to heat or dryness.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Wilted corsage dangles from my wrist
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'wilted' to describe the corsage, symbolizing the loss of innocence and the passing of time, as well as the fragility of relationships.

vocab.definition: awkward means causing or feeling embarrassment, unease, or discomfort, often due to a lack of social skill or a difficult situation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Don't make it awkward in second period
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'awkward' to express the fear of making things uncomfortable or embarrassing in a social situation, highlighting the importance of being genuine and true to oneself.

vocab.definition: regret means feeling sad or unhappy about something that has happened or been done, often because it cannot be changed or undone.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Better that than regret it for all time
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'regret' to express the idea that it's better to take risks and face the consequences than to live with the what-ifs and maybes of a missed opportunity.

songs.title: Actually Romantic

High-fived my ex and then you said you're glad he ghosted me
Wrote me a song saying it makes you sick to see my face
Some people might be offended

But it's actually sweet
All the time you've spent on me
It's honestly wild
All the effort you've put in
It's actually romantic
I really gotta hand it to you, ooh

But you keep sending me funny valentines
And I know you think it comes off vicious
But it's precious, adorable
Like a toy chihuahua barking at me from a tiny purse
That's how much it hurts
How many times has your boyfriend said
"Why are we always talking 'bout her?"

It's actually sweet
All the time you've spent on me
It's honestly wild
All the effort you've put in
It's actually romantic
I really gotta hand it to you, ooh
No man has ever loved me like you do

You think I'm tacky, baby
Stop talking dirty to me
It sounded nasty, but it feels like you're flirting with me
I mind my business, God's my witness that I don't provoke it

All the time you've spent on me (Ah)
It's honestly lovely

It's actually romantic
Really gotta hand it to you, to you
No man has ever loved me like you do

It's actually romantic (You've just given me so much attention; la-la-la-la)
It's actually romantic
(It's so) Shoo-wop-bop, romantic

vocab.definition: having or showing a cruel and violent nature
vocab.lyric_snippet: And I know you think it comes off vicious
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her perception of the speaker's intentions, implying that their actions might be hurtful or aggressive.

vocab.definition: having or showing a poor sense of taste or style
vocab.lyric_snippet: You think I'm tacky, baby
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe how she perceives the speaker's opinion of her, implying that they find her unrefined or unsophisticated.

vocab.definition: the act of behaving in a way that is intended to attract someone's attention or interest, especially in a romantic or sexual way
vocab.lyric_snippet: It sounded nasty, but it feels like you're flirting with me
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the speaker's behavior, suggesting that their words or actions are intended to be seductive or charming.

vocab.definition: caused or encouraged to feel or do something
vocab.lyric_snippet: I mind my business, God's my witness that I don't provoke it
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her actions, implying that she is not intentionally trying to elicit a reaction from the speaker.

vocab.definition: extremely attractive or charming
vocab.lyric_snippet: But it's precious, adorable
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the speaker's behavior, suggesting that it is endearing or charming.

vocab.definition: valued or cherished highly
vocab.lyric_snippet: But it's precious, adorable
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the speaker's behavior, suggesting that it is valuable or meaningful to her.

vocab.definition: the work or energy that is needed to do something
vocab.lyric_snippet: All the effort you've put in

vocab.definition: in a cruel or violent way
vocab.lyric_snippet: And I know you think it comes off vicious
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the speaker's intentions, implying that their actions might be hurtful or aggressive.

You caught me off my guard
I hope I get what I want (Get what I want)
'Cause I know what I want

(I just, I just) I just want you, huh (Baby)

vocab.definition: A helicopter, especially one used for transportation or recreation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They want that yacht life, under chopper blades

vocab.definition: A high-end fashion brand, known for its luxurious and stylish clothing.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They want those bright lights and Balenci' shades
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'Balenci'' to describe a luxurious and fashionable lifestyle.

vocab.definition: Relating to or expressing a judgment or opinion about the quality or value of something.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They want that critical smash Palme d'Or
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'critical' to describe a prestigious and highly-regarded award.

vocab.definition: A prestigious award given to the best film at the Cannes Film Festival.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They want that critical smash Palme d'Or
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'Palme d'Or' to describe a highly-regarded and prestigious award.

vocab.definition: People who claim to have moral principles or values but do not practice what they preach.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And that good surf, no hypocrites
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'hypocrites' to describe people who pretend to be something they're not.

vocab.definition: A network of intersecting lines or paths, especially one used for navigation or transportation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They want that freedom, living off the grid
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'grid' to describe a lifestyle of freedom and independence.

vocab.definition: Exciting, fun, or wild.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They want that spring break that was fucking lit
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'lit' to describe an exciting and wild experience.

vocab.definition: A desire or hope for something to happen or exist.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Got a wish (Wish) list (List)
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'wish' to describe a desire or hope for something.

vocab.definition: feeling or showing great unhappiness or anxiety; extremely upset
vocab.lyric_snippet: Daisy's bare naked, I was distraught
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her emotional state when she sees Daisy's bare naked body, implying a sense of shock and distress.

vocab.definition: having or showing a belief in luck or chance, especially in a way that is not based on reason or science
vocab.lyric_snippet: And, baby, I'll admit I've been a little superstitious
vocab.context: Taylor admits to being superstitious, implying that she has a tendency to believe in luck or chance, which is a recurring theme in the song.

vocab.definition: having bad luck; experiencing misfortune or bad fortune
vocab.lyric_snippet: Penny's unlucky, I took him back
vocab.context: Taylor describes Penny as unlucky, implying that Penny has a tendency to experience bad luck or misfortune.

vocab.definition: past tense of 'amaze', meaning to surprise or astonish someone greatly
vocab.lyric_snippet: He ah-matized me and opened my eyes
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe how her partner surprised or astonished her, implying a sense of wonder or amazement.

vocab.definition: a type of large evergreen tree native to the western coast of North America
vocab.lyric_snippet: Redwood tree, it ain't hard to see
vocab.context: Taylor uses the redwood tree as a metaphor for her partner's love, implying that it is strong and enduring.

vocab.definition: having or showing an attitude of pride or self-confidence, often in a way that is considered arrogant or off-putting
vocab.lyric_snippet: Forgive me, it sounds cocky
vocab.context: Taylor acknowledges that her statement sounds cocky, implying that she is aware of her own pride or self-confidence.

vocab.definition: the state of being a man, or the qualities or characteristics that are considered typical of men
vocab.lyric_snippet: New Heights (New Heights) of manhood (Manhood)
vocab.context: Taylor uses the term 'manhood' to describe her partner's growth or maturity, implying that he has reached a new level of understanding or awareness.

songs.title: Cancelled!
songs.lyrics:
You thought that it would be okay, at first
The situation could be saved, of course
But they'd already picked out your grave and hearse
Beware the wrath of masked crusaders

Did you girlboss too close to the sun?
Did they catch you having far too much fun?
Come with me, when they see us, they'll run
Something wicked this way comes

Good thing I like my friends cancelled
I like 'em cloaked in Gucci and in scandal
Like my whiskey sour, and poison thorny flowers
Welcome to my underworld where it gets quite dark
At least you know exactly who your friends are
They're the ones with matching scars

It's easy to love you when you're popular
The optics click, everyone prospers
But one single drop, you're off the roster
"Tone-deaf and hot, let's fucking off her"

Did you make a joke only a man could?
Were you just too smug for your own good?
Or bring a tiny violin to a knife fight?
Baby, that all ends tonight

Good thing I like my friends cancelled (Cancelled)
I like 'em cloaked in Gucci and in scandal (Yeah)
Like my whiskey sour, and poison thorny flowers
(Honey) Welcome to my underworld, it'll break your heart
At least you know exactly who your friends are
They're the ones with matching scars

They stood by me before my exoneration
They believed I was innocent
So I'm not here for judgment, no

But if you can't be good, then just be better at it
Everyone's got bodies in the attic
Or took somebody's man, we'll take you by the hand
And soon, you'll learn the art of never getting caught

It's a good thing I like my friends cancelled
(You know that) I like 'em cloaked in Gucci and in scandal
I like my whiskey sour, and poison thorny flowers (Ooh)
Yeah, it's a good thing I like my friends cancelled (Cancelled)
I salute you if you're much too much to handle
Like my whiskey sour (I like it), and poison thorny flowers (I love it)
Can't you see my infamy loves company?
Now they've broken you like they've broken me
But a shattered glass is a lot more sharp
And now you know exactly who your friends are (You know who we are)
We're the ones with matching scars

vocab.definition: a hole in the ground for a person who has died
vocab.lyric_snippet: But they'd already picked out your grave and hearse
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the finality and seriousness of the situation, implying that the person's fate is sealed.

vocab.definition: a woman who is confident and successful in her career, often in a traditionally male-dominated field
vocab.lyric_snippet: Did you girlboss too close to the sun?
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone who is overconfident and reckless, implying that they have become too big for their own good.

vocab.definition: the way something appears to others, especially in terms of how it is perceived or interpreted
vocab.lyric_snippet: It's easy to love you when you're popular, The optics click, everyone prospers
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe how people perceive and react to someone's popularity, implying that it can be superficial and fleeting.

vocab.definition: lacking sensitivity or awareness of the feelings or needs of others
vocab.lyric_snippet: Tone-deaf and hot, let's fucking off her
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone who is insensitive and unaware of the impact of their words or actions on others.

vocab.definition: the act of clearing someone of a crime or wrongdoing
vocab.lyric_snippet: They stood by me before my exoneration
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the process of being cleared of a false accusation, implying a sense of vindication and relief.

vocab.definition: a state of being notorious or infamous, often due to a person's bad behavior or reputation
vocab.lyric_snippet: Can't you see my infamy loves company?
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her own reputation and the company she keeps, implying a sense of pride and acceptance.

vocab.definition: broken or split into pieces, often in a violent or sudden manner
vocab.lyric_snippet: But a shattered glass is a lot more sharp
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the aftermath of a traumatic event, implying a sense of fragility and vulnerability.

vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe how people used to call her 'sweetheart' in a way that was condescending or manipulative.

vocab.definition: Intended to influence or control someone's behavior or emotions in a way that is unfair or deceitful.

vocab.context: Taylor implies that the woman was being manipulative by telling her to back off because her man had looked at Taylor.

vocab.definition: Showing an attitude of patronizing superiority, often in a way that is insulting or humiliating.
vocab.lyric_snippet: When anyone called me 'sweetheart' It was passive-aggressive at the bar
vocab.context: Taylor suggests that people used to call her 'sweetheart' in a condescending way, implying that she was inferior.

vocab.definition: A woman who is considered to be unpleasant, disagreeable, or difficult to deal with.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And the bitch was telling me to back off
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a woman who is being confrontational and aggressive.

vocab.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse, often in a casual or promiscuous manner.
vocab.lyric_snippet: He was screwing around with my mind
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone who is emotionally manipulating her.

vocab.definition: To influence or control someone's behavior or emotions in a way that is unfair or deceitful.
vocab.lyric_snippet: He was screwing around with my mind Asking, 'What are you wearing?', too high
vocab.context: Taylor implies that someone was manipulating her by asking her questions that were intended to make her feel uncomfortable or insecure.

vocab.definition: Showing an attitude of patronizing superiority, often in a way that is insulting or humiliating.

songs.title: The Life of a Showgirl
songs.lyrics:
Her name was Kitty
Made her money being pretty and witty
They gave her the keys to this city
Then they said she didn't do it legitly, oh
I bought a ticket
She's dancing in her garters and fishnets
Fifty in the cast, zero missteps
Looking back, I guess it was kismet

I waited by the stage door, packed in with the autograph hounds
Barking her name, then glowing like the end of a cigarette
Wow, she came out
I said, "You're living my drеam"
Then she said to me

"Hеy, thank you for the lovely bouquet
You're sweeter than a peach
But you don't know the life of a showgirl, babe
And you're never ever gonna
Wait, the more you play, the more that you pay
You're softer than a kitten
So you don't know the life of a showgirl, babe
And you're never gonna wanna"

She was a menace
The baby of the family in Lenox
Her father whored around like all men did
Her mother took pills and played tennis

So she waited by the stage door as the club promoter arrived
She said, "I'd sell my soul to have a taste of a magnificent life that's all mine"
But that's not what showgirls get
They leave us for dead
[Chorus: Sabrina Carpenter, 
Sabrina Carpenter & Taylor Swift
]
Hey
, thank you for the lovely bouquet
You're sweeter than a peach
But 
you don't know the life of a showgirl, babe
And you're never ever gonna
Wait, the more you play, 
the more that you pay
You're softer than a kitten
So you don't know the life of a showgirl, babe
And you're never gonna wanna
[Bridge: Taylor Swift &

]
I took her pearls of wisdom, hung them from my neck
I paid my dues with every bruise, I knew what to expect
Do you wanna take a skate on the ice inside my veins?
They ripped me off like false lashes and then threw me away
And all the headshots on the walls
Of the dance hall are of the bitches
Who wish I'd hurry up and die
But I'm immortal now, baby dolls
I couldn't if I tried
So I say
[Chorus: Taylor Swift & 
Sabrina Carpenter
]
"Thank you for the lovely bouquet
I'm married to the hustle
And now I know the life of a showgirl, babe
And I'll never know another
Pain hidden by the lipstick and lace (
Lipstick and lace
)
Sequins are forever
And now I know the life of a showgirl, babe
Wouldn't have it any other way" (Wouldn't have it any other way)

(Thank you for the lovely bouquet)
Oh, wouldn't have it any other way
(Thank you for the lovely bouquet)
Hey, Kitty
(Thank you for the lovely bouquet)
Now I make my money being pretty and witty
Thank you for the lovely bouquet

]
Thank you for an unforgettable night
We will see you next time
Give it up for the band
And the dancers
And of course, Sabrina
I love you, Taylor
That's our show, we love you so much
Good night

vocab.definition: a person or thing that seems to have been destined or meant to be, often in a romantic or fortunate way
vocab.lyric_snippet: Looking back, I guess it was kismet
vocab.context: Taylor uses kismet to describe the connection between her and the showgirl Kitty, suggesting a destined or romantic encounter

vocab.definition: a person or thing that causes danger or harm, often in a threatening or intimidating way
vocab.lyric_snippet: She was a menace
vocab.context: Taylor describes Kitty as a menace, possibly due to her father's whoring and her mother's pill-taking, suggesting a troubled or unstable upbringing

vocab.definition: extremely impressive or beautiful, often in a grand or spectacular way
vocab.lyric_snippet: a magnificent life that's all mine
vocab.context: Kitty expresses her desire for a magnificent life, suggesting a sense of aspiration or ambition

vocab.definition: alive forever, often in a figurative or metaphorical sense
vocab.lyric_snippet: But I'm immortal now, baby dolls
vocab.context: Taylor describes herself as immortal, possibly due to her experiences as a showgirl, suggesting a sense of resilience or invincibility

vocab.definition: women who are considered to be cruel, unpleasant, or unkind
vocab.lyric_snippet: Of the dance hall are of the bitches
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'bitches' to describe women who are jealous or resentful of her success, suggesting a sense of rivalry or competition

vocab.definition: a person or thing that is constantly active or busy, often in a way that is exhausting or demanding
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm married to the hustle
vocab.context: Taylor describes herself as married to the hustle, suggesting a sense of dedication or commitment to her career as a showgirl

vocab.definition: small, shiny pieces of metal or plastic used to decorate clothing or other objects
vocab.lyric_snippet: Sequins are forever
vocab.context: Taylor uses sequins as a metaphor for the glamour or spectacle of showgirl culture, suggesting a sense of permanence or timelessness
